VAL-DEPLOY-001.evidence: gcloud run services describe confirms service 'ornn-api' deployed in project 'ornn-benchmarking' region 'us-east1', status Ready=True. curl GET /health returned HTTP 200 with body {"status":"ok","version":"0.1.0","service":"Ornn Benchmarking API"}.
VAL-DEPLOY-001.details: Service URL: https://ornn-api-jt6edideba-ue.a.run.app. Created at 2026-02-28T05:58:08Z. Latest revision: ornn-api-00002-5d4. All status conditions True (Ready, ConfigurationsReady, RoutesReady).

VAL-DEPLOY-002.evidence: POST /api/v1/runs with X-API-Key: dev-test-key returned HTTP 201 with body {"run_id":"53fa7dcc0d414d729d4e866cf508938a","received_at":"2026-02-28T06:29:53.937651+00:00","stored_at":"2026-02-28T06:29:53.937651+00:00"}.
VAL-DEPLOY-002.details: Submitted test payload with report_id 'deploy-val-001' containing schema_version 1.0.0, system_inventory, scores, and empty sections/manifest. Received run_id and server timestamps confirming persistence.

steps.action: POST /api/v1/runs with valid API key and test payload

VAL-DEPLOY-003.evidence: POST without API key returned HTTP 401 {"detail":"Missing API key. Provide a valid key via the X-API-Key header."}. POST with invalid key (X-API-Key: invalid-key-xyz) returned HTTP 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized."}.
VAL-DEPLOY-003.details: Both missing and invalid API key scenarios correctly rejected with 401. No sensitive internal details leaked in response bodies.

steps.observed: HTTP 401, detail: Missing API key message

VAL-DEPLOY-004.evidence: GET /api/v1/runs/53fa7dcc0d414d729d4e866cf508938a with valid API key returned HTTP 200. Response payload matches submitted data: report_id=deploy-val-001, schema_version=1.0.0, scores.ornn_i=100.0, scores.ornn_t=100.0, scores.qualification=Premium, system_inventory.gpus[0].name=Test GPU.
VAL-DEPLOY-004.details: Round-trip verified: all submitted fields present in retrieval response. Server added run_id, received_at, stored_at, and dedupe_key fields.

steps.observed: HTTP 200, payload matches submitted data with server-added fields

VAL-DEPLOY-005.evidence: 1) Run submitted in VAL-DEPLOY-002 was successfully retrieved in VAL-DEPLOY-004, proving persistence across requests. 2) gcloud env check confirms FIRESTORE_PROJECT_ID=ornn-benchmarking is set as container env var, confirming Firestore is the storage backend. 3) deploy.sh sets --set-env-vars=FIRESTORE_PROJECT_ID=${GCP_PROJECT} in the gcloud run deploy command.
VAL-DEPLOY-005.details: Firestore persistence confirmed through: successful round-trip retrieval of submitted run, FIRESTORE_PROJECT_ID env var set on the live service, and deploy.sh configuration using Firestore as sole database.

steps.action: Verify retrieval of submitted run (from VAL-DEPLOY-004)
steps.observed: Run successfully retrieved with all original fields

steps.action: gcloud run services describe --format env check

steps.expected: Firestore env var set in deployment

VAL-DEPLOY-006.evidence: gcloud run services describe shows: no minScale annotation (defaults to 0 = scale-to-zero), maxScale=2, memory=256Mi, cpu=1. deploy.sh explicitly sets MIN_INSTANCES=0 with comment 'Scale-to-zero: no cost when idle'. Dockerfile uses only python:3.12-slim + pip install. services.yaml lists only Cloud Run (api) and Firestore emulator. No VMs, Cloud SQL, GKE, or other paid always-on infrastructure in any config.
VAL-DEPLOY-006.details: Free-tier-safe configuration confirmed: Cloud Run with scale-to-zero (min-instances=0), Firestore as only database, no always-on infrastructure. deploy.sh header explicitly states 'Free-tier-safe defaults' and documents constraints.

steps.action: Check min-instances in gcloud describe output

steps.expected: Only Cloud Run + Firestore, no VMs/SQL/GKE
steps.observed: MIN_INSTANCES=0, only gcloud run deploy + Firestore env var, no other infra

VAL-CROSS-004.evidence: Default deployment path (deploy.sh) uses only: 1) Cloud Run managed platform with --min-instances=0 (scale-to-zero), 2) Firestore native mode as sole database (FIRESTORE_PROJECT_ID env var). No VMs, Cloud SQL, GKE, Redis, or other always-on paid infrastructure. services.yaml confirms only Cloud Run API + Firestore emulator for local dev. Dockerfile contains no references to external paid services.
VAL-CROSS-004.details: Free-tier-safe baseline confirmed across all config files: deploy.sh (Cloud Run + Firestore only), Dockerfile (minimal Python container), services.yaml (only 2 services: api on 8080, firestore-emulator on 8085). deploy.sh header explicitly documents free-tier-safe design intent.

steps.observed: Only api (Cloud Run) and firestore-emulator listed

steps.action: Verify gcloud describe output for paid infra
steps.observed: No minScale annotation (defaults to 0), maxScale=2, managed platform

summary: Tested 7 assertions: all 7 passed. Live Cloud Run service is deployed and reachable (VAL-DEPLOY-001), accepts authenticated submissions returning 201 (VAL-DEPLOY-002), rejects missing/invalid API keys with 401 (VAL-DEPLOY-003), supports retrieval round-trip with payload matching (VAL-DEPLOY-004), persists data in Firestore (VAL-DEPLOY-005), and uses free-tier-safe scale-to-zero configuration with no always-on paid infrastructure (VAL-DEPLOY-006, VAL-CROSS-004).
